title: Luminous Portuguese Fish Broth
description: A mustardy, glassy-jelly broth designed for sick-day comfort. This Portuguese-inspired fish stew uses mirin and no-onion-no-garlic ingredients for a gentle, soothing flavor.
instructions: Place water, kombu, and mirin in a saucepan and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low and add the fish fillets, letting them cook for eight minutes until opaque.
Stir in soy sauce, mustard, lemon juice, salt, and pepper, then simmer for another two minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the broth cool slightly before adding agar-agar, stirring until fully dissolved.
Transfer the mixture to a shallow dish and refrigerate for at least one hour or until the broth sets into a glassy jelly.
Slice the set broth into bite-sized cubes and serve on a chilled plate.
